storyBeat: 世界上沒有故事 — 蜘蛛想去拿

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Here are 4 words you will meet in tonight's story.
questions.explanationZh:
本節新單字 (左中右英):
🔑 story = 故事
🔑 night = 夜晚
🔑 sky = 天空
🔑 spider = 蜘蛛
背熟這 4 個字,故事就會輕鬆聽懂。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Long ago, there were no stories in the world.
questions.explanationZh: 很久以前,世界上沒有故事。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: People sat together at night, and no one had a tale to tell.
questions.question: What was night-time like for the people?
questions.explanationZh: 推理: no tale to tell → 很安靜很空 (paraphrase)。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: All the stories belonged to Nyame, the Sky God.
questions.questionEn: Could people on the ground share any story?
questions.explanationZh: 推理: 故事全屬天神 → 地上人沒得分 → 答 No

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: A small spider named Anansi looked up at the sky.
questions.explanationZh: 一隻名叫 Anansi 的小蜘蛛抬頭看著天空。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Anansi was tiny, but his head was full of plans.
questions.question: What kind of spider was Anansi?
questions.explanationZh: 推理: tiny + head full of plans → 小但點子多 (paraphrase)。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He wanted everyone on the ground to have a story to share.
questions.explanationZh: 他希望地上每個人都有故事可以分享。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: So he made a long thin thread that went all the way up.
questions.question: What did Anansi make to reach the sky?
questions.explanationZh: 推理: long thin thread → 高高的絲繩 (paraphrase, X3-safe)。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The thread shone in the moonlight like a silver road.
questions.explanationZh: 那條線在月光下像一條銀色的路。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: How did Anansi feel as he started his climb?
questions.question: How did Anansi feel?
questions.explanationZh: 小蜘蛛主動爬上天 → 勇敢準備好。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Anansi put one foot on the silver thread and began to climb...
questions.explanationZh: Anansi 把一隻腳放上銀線,開始往上爬……

storyBeat: 天神開價 — 抓三個動物

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Here are 4 words you will meet in tonight's story.
questions.explanationZh:
本節新單字 (左中右英):
🔑 sky god = 天神
🔑 buy = 買
🔑 price = 價錢
🔑 animal = 動物
背熟這 4 個字,故事就會輕鬆聽懂。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Anansi went up the long thread and stood before Nyame.
questions.explanationZh: Anansi 順著長線爬上去,站在 Nyame 面前。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He bent his small head low to greet the Sky God.
questions.question: How did Anansi greet Nyame?
questions.explanationZh: 推理: bent small head low → 低頭鞠躬 (paraphrase)。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Anansi spoke in a soft, polite voice to the Sky God.
questions.questionEn: Did Anansi try to be respectful?
questions.explanationZh: 推理: soft polite voice → 想表現尊重 → 答 Yes

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "I want to buy all your stories," Anansi said.
questions.explanationZh: 「我想買下你所有的故事,」Anansi 說。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Nyame looked down and laughed at the small spider.
questions.question: What did Nyame do at first?
options: made fun of the tiny visitor
questions.explanationZh: 推理: looked down + laughed → 取笑這個小訪客 (paraphrase, X3-safe)。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "The price is high," Nyame said. "Bring me three animals."
questions.explanationZh: 「價錢很高,」Nyame 說。「給我帶三個動物來。」

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The list was a swarm of hornets, a long python, and a leopard.
questions.question: What was on the list?
questions.explanationZh: 推理: hornets + python + leopard → 三個強的動物 (paraphrase)。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "I can do this," Anansi said. He did not even blink.
questions.explanationZh: 「我可以辦到,」Anansi 說。他連眼睛都沒眨。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The Sky God set the price, and the spider said yes right away.
questions.question: What is this scene mainly about?
options: a deal between a god and a spider
questions.explanationZh: 主旨 = 天神和蜘蛛的交易。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Anansi climbed down to the ground and looked at the list in his hand...
questions.explanationZh: Anansi 爬回地上,看著手中的清單……

storyBeat: 抓蜂 — 用一片葉子和一個鍋

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Here are 4 words you will meet in tonight's story.
questions.explanationZh:
本節新單字 (左中右英):
🔑 hornet = 蜜蜂 (Hornet)
🔑 leaf = 葉子
🔑 roof = 屋頂
🔑 pot = 鍋
背熟這 4 個字,故事就會輕鬆聽懂。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The first animal was a group of hornets in a big tree.
questions.explanationZh: 第一個動物是住在大樹上的一群蜂。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The tree was full of a loud, busy buzzing sound.
questions.question: What did the tree sound like?
questions.explanationZh: 推理: loud busy buzzing → 吵又忙的嗡嗡聲 (paraphrase)。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The other animals walked far around the tree, not close to it.
questions.questionEn: Was the hornet tree a safe place for most animals?
questions.explanationZh: 推理: 大家繞遠走 → 不安全 → 答 No

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Anansi cut a big leaf and held it over his head like a roof.
questions.explanationZh: Anansi 剪了一大片葉子,舉在頭上像屋頂。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Now he had a small green roof on top of his head.
questions.question: Why was the leaf over his head?
options: to keep something off his head
questions.explanationZh: 推理: roof on top → 擋住東西 (paraphrase)。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He filled a pot with water and poured some on the hornet tree.
questions.explanationZh: 他裝滿一鍋水,把水倒在蜂的樹上。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The hornets thought a sudden rain had come too soon.
questions.question: What did the hornets think was happening?
questions.explanationZh: 推理: thought rain came → 下雨來了 (paraphrase)。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "The rain is coming! Quick, get in my pot!" Anansi called.
questions.explanationZh: 「下雨了!快進我的鍋!」Anansi 喊。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: What did the hornets do when they heard him?
questions.question: What did the hornets do?
questions.explanationZh: 怕雨 + 聽到喊 → 衝進鍋裡。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Anansi put a lid on the pot. The hornets had given up and gone inside...
questions.explanationZh: Anansi 把蓋子蓋上。蜂全都放棄,進到鍋裡了……

storyBeat: 抓蛇 — 用一根樹枝和一個聰明話

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Here are 4 words you will meet in tonight's story.
questions.explanationZh:
本節新單字 (左中右英):
🔑 python = 大蛇 (Python)
🔑 stick = 樹枝
🔑 friend = 朋友
🔑 tie = 綁
背熟這 4 個字,故事就會輕鬆聽懂。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The second animal was a long python in the river grass.
questions.explanationZh: 第二個動物是河邊草叢裡的一條長蛇。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: His body went on and on, like a green road in the grass.
questions.question: How was the python's body?
questions.explanationZh: 推理: on and on, like a road → 很長像一條路 (paraphrase)。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The python often showed off his long body to the other animals.
questions.questionEn: Was the python proud of how long he was?
questions.explanationZh: 推理: 常炫耀身長 → 很驕傲 → 答 Yes

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Anansi found a strong stick and brought it to the python.
questions.explanationZh: Anansi 找了一根結實的樹枝,拿到大蛇面前。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "My friend says you are not really that long," Anansi said.
questions.question: What did Anansi tell the python?
options: that some friend doubted his length
questions.explanationZh: 推理: friend says not that long → 朋友懷疑長度 (paraphrase)。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The python lifted his head. His eyes went sharp and bright.
questions.explanationZh: 大蛇抬起頭。他的眼睛變得銳利又閃亮。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He wanted to show everyone how very long he really was.
questions.question: How did the python feel after hearing Anansi?
questions.explanationZh: 推理: wanted to show how long → 想要炫耀 (paraphrase)。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The python lay down by the stick to show his full length.
questions.explanationZh: 大蛇躺在樹枝旁,要展現完整的長度。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: What did Anansi do while the python was lying still?
questions.question: What did Anansi do next?
questions.explanationZh: 蛇躺好 + Anansi 有樹枝 → 把他綁在樹枝上。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The python tried to move, but the stick held him safely. Anansi smiled...
questions.explanationZh: 大蛇想動,但樹枝把他穩穩固定住。Anansi 笑了……

storyBeat: 抓豹 — 挖一個洞,等夜晚

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Here are 4 words you will meet in tonight's story.
questions.explanationZh:
本節新單字 (左中右英):
🔑 leopard = 豹 (Leopard)
🔑 dig = 挖
🔑 hole = 洞
🔑 rope = 繩子
背熟這 4 個字,故事就會輕鬆聽懂。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The third animal was a leopard with sharp, bright eyes.
questions.explanationZh: 第三個動物是一隻眼睛銳利明亮的豹。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: No animal in the forest dared to walk near his rest tree.
questions.question: Was the leopard easy to catch?
questions.explanationZh: 推理: no animal dared near → 大家都離很遠 (paraphrase)。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: For two days, Anansi watched which path the leopard used at night.
questions.questionEn: Did Anansi plan his trap with care?
questions.explanationZh: 推理: 觀察兩天 → 仔細計畫 → 答 Yes

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Anansi dug a deep hole on the path the leopard liked.
questions.explanationZh: Anansi 在豹喜歡走的路上挖了一個深洞。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He covered the hole with leaves so no one could see it.
questions.question: Why did Anansi put leaves on top?
options: to hide the hole from view
questions.explanationZh: 推理: cover so no one sees → 讓人看不到 (paraphrase)。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: That night the leopard walked the path with quiet, strong feet.
questions.explanationZh: 那天晚上,豹用安靜有力的腳走過那條路。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: One step on the leaves, and his big body went straight down.
questions.question: What happened to the leopard?
questions.explanationZh: 推理: body went straight down → 掉進洞裡 (paraphrase)。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The leopard tried to climb out, but the walls were too high.
questions.explanationZh: 豹想爬出來,但洞壁太高了。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Anansi looked down at him and held out a long, strong rope.
questions.question: Why did Anansi bring a rope?
options: to lift the leopard up safely
questions.explanationZh: 推理:豹在洞底 + Anansi 拿繩 → 要把豹安全拉上來。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "I will help you up," Anansi said. The leopard looked up at the rope...
questions.explanationZh: 「我幫你上來,」Anansi 說。豹抬頭看著繩子……

storyBeat: 蜘蛛全帶回天神 — 天神給故事

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Here are 4 words you will meet in tonight's story.
questions.explanationZh:
本節新單字 (左中右英):
🔑 bring = 帶
🔑 head = 頭
🔑 box = 盒子
🔑 world = 世界
背熟這 4 個字,故事就會輕鬆聽懂。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Anansi climbed up the thread again, and this time he was not alone.
questions.explanationZh: Anansi 又爬上那條線,這次他不是一個人。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Behind him came the hornets, the long python, and the spotted leopard.
questions.question: Who came up with Anansi?
questions.explanationZh: 推理: hornets + python + leopard → 抓到的三個動物 (paraphrase)。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Nyame slowly stood up from his big chair to take a closer look.
questions.questionEn: Was the Sky God surprised by what he saw?
questions.explanationZh: 推理: 站起來 + 近看 → 驚訝 → 答 Yes

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The Sky God looked at the hornets, the python, and the leopard.
questions.explanationZh: 天神看著蜂、大蛇,還有豹。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He could not see any rope on his back or any cut on his skin.
questions.question: What did Nyame notice about the catch?
questions.explanationZh: 推理: no rope + no cut → 沒有動物受傷 (paraphrase)。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "You are small, but your head is big," Nyame said with a smile.
questions.explanationZh: 「你很小,但腦袋很大,」Nyame 笑著說。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He meant Anansi had used his clever ideas, not his strong arms.
questions.question: What did Nyame mean by "your head is big"?
questions.explanationZh: 推理: clever ideas not strong arms → 很聰明 (paraphrase)。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He gave Anansi a wooden box full of all the stories of the world.
questions.explanationZh: 他給了 Anansi 一個木盒,裡面裝著全世界所有的故事。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: How did Anansi feel when he held the box?
questions.question: How did Anansi feel?
questions.explanationZh: 小蜘蛛拿到全世界的故事 → 驕傲又開心。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Anansi held the box close to his chest and looked down at the world below...
questions.explanationZh: Anansi 把盒子抱在胸前,看著下面的世界……

storyBeat: 蜘蛛把故事散到全世界

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Here are 4 words you will meet in tonight's story.
questions.explanationZh:
本節新單字 (左中右英):
🔑 carry = 抱
🔑 open = 打開
🔑 lid = 蓋子
🔑 fly = 飛
背熟這 4 個字,故事就會輕鬆聽懂。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Anansi carried the heavy box back down to the ground.
questions.explanationZh: Anansi 把重重的盒子帶回地上。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Every step down the thread was slow and careful.
questions.question: How did Anansi move on the way down?
questions.explanationZh: 推理: slow and careful → 慢慢小心 (paraphrase)。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: In the village, the people came out and made a circle around him.
questions.questionEn: Were the people curious about what was in the box?
questions.explanationZh: 推理: 圍成一圈 → 很好奇 → 答 Yes

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He opened the lid in the middle of the village.
questions.explanationZh: 他在村子中央把蓋子打開。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The stories flew out like birds and went in every direction.
questions.question: What did the stories look like?
questions.explanationZh: 推理: flew out like birds → 鳥飛出來 (paraphrase)。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: They went to every corner of the earth, near and far.
questions.explanationZh: 它們飛到地球的每個角落,近的遠的都有。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Now every home, even small ones, had a story by the fire at night.
questions.question: What changed for the people at night?
options: every home had a story to share
questions.explanationZh: 推理: every home had a story → 每家都有故事可分享 (paraphrase)。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: And that is why, even today, there is a tale in every home.
questions.explanationZh: 所以,直到今天,每個家裡都有故事。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: A small spider with a big head changed the night for everyone.
questions.question: What is the main lesson of this story?
options: clever thinking can beat brute strength
questions.explanationZh: 主旨 = 小蜘蛛用聰明拿到所有故事 → 聰明可以勝過蠻力。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Tonight your story is also one of Anansi's. What is the first story you remember?
questions.explanationZh: 今晚你聽的故事,也是 Anansi 帶回來的。你記得的第一個故事是什麼?
